Includes supportive nonpharmacologic measures for sicca symptoms (hydration, sugar-free gum/lozenges, saliva substitutes, dental caries prevention; artificial tears and eye protection) even if medication is prescribed.
Uses patient-centered communication (checks understanding, invites questions) and avoids overclaiming (does not state Sjogren is infectious or cured by pilocarpine).

minimal_paths.description: Diagnose Sjogren syndrome and start pilocarpine for xerostomia after completing critical safety checks and counseling.
must_collect: history of glaucoma (especially narrow-angle) or significant eye disease
cardiac history (arrhythmia/conduction disease) and baseline vitals if available
steps: Explain that Sjogren syndrome is an autoimmune condition causing gland dysfunction leading to dry mouth and dry eyes.
Confirm no allergy to pilocarpine (or relevant components) before prescribing.
Check for clinically significant drug interactions with current medications before prescribing.

Check contraindications/precautions (e.g., uncontrolled asthma/COPD with bronchospasm, narrow-angle glaucoma, significant cardiac conduction disease) before prescribing; if present, avoid pilocarpine and pivot to alternatives.
Prescribe pilocarpine for xerostomia and counsel on dosing approach and common side effects (notably sweating) and warning symptoms.

Recommend adjunctive measures: saliva substitutes, sugar-free gum/lozenges, hydration, dental fluoride/caries prevention; artificial tears for dry eyes and consider ophthalmology evaluation if severe.
Provide follow-up expectations (symptom response and tolerability) and when to seek urgent care (wheezing/shortness of breath, severe dizziness/syncope, acute eye pain/vision changes).

acceptable_variants.description: Treat xerostomia with pilocarpine while emphasizing conservative measures first and using shared decision-making about starting medication now vs after trial of saliva substitutes.
steps: Explain Sjogren syndrome as an autoimmune cause of sicca symptoms.
Discuss nonpharmacologic options and patient preference for symptom control.
Complete allergy, interaction, and contraindication checks prior to prescribing.
Prescribe pilocarpine if appropriate; counsel on adverse effects and red flags.
Reinforce dental/eye supportive care and arrange reassessment timeframe.

acceptable_variants.description: If pilocarpine is unsafe due to contraindication or interaction, avoid prescribing and provide an alternative symptomatic plan (no medication tool call required beyond the attempted decision), documenting rationale in counseling.
steps: Explain Sjogren syndrome as an autoimmune condition and link to symptoms.
Complete allergy, interaction, and contraindication checks and identify the reason pilocarpine is not appropriate.
Offer alternatives (e.g., saliva substitutes, sugar-free gum/lozenges, hydration, dental fluoride; consider cevimeline if appropriate and available; optimize contributing meds causing dryness).
Provide safety-net advice and follow-up plan for reassessment of symptoms and complications.
